workflows.name: BugDB
workflows.description: Read, comment, create, or update BugDB bugs.
workflows.context: BugDB-only workflow. Use BugDB MCP first for existing bug reads, summaries, comments, and field updates when the needed MCP tool exists. If the MCP session cannot update existing bug fields, an approved BugDB field update may use the dev-bugdb-helper skill CLI at ~/.codex/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh. Use /bin/python3.11 only through that skill wrapper, do not change global python3, do not print OAuth tokens or client secrets, read the current bug/header fields before mutation, and resolve friendly field labels such as Bug Fix ETA, Bug Fix By, or Component Version to exact BugDB API keys before constructing the update payload. For product_id 2419, use cached metadata at docs/bugdb_header_fields_2419.json; call header-fields only if the cache is missing or does not contain the requested label. Do not guess field keys. Update only the requested fields while preserving required BugDB fields, then verify with a readback and rerun any gated validation. Use the local bugdb_create_bug.py helper only for new bug creation.

operations.name: Summarize Bug
operations.description: Retrieve the bug through BugDB MCP and provide a concise summary.

operations.name: Add Private Comment
operations.description: Retrieve the bug through BugDB MCP, then add the requested hidden/private comment through MCP.

operations.name: Create Bug
operations.description: Create a new BugDB bug using the local helper. Defaults come from bugdb_create_payload.json.

operations.name: Custom BugDB Task
operations.description: Run a custom BugDB-only request. Existing bugs must use MCP where possible.

workflows.name: ADE
workflows.description: ADE view, transaction, OraReview, merge request, and cleanup workflows.
workflows.context: ADE-only workflow. Use the ade-lens, orareview, and mergereq skills where relevant. If a selected skill reports a newer version, install it before continuing without asking for separate approval. Use CodeSearch, OraReview, and Mergereq MCP tools for read-only lookup and report active view, active transaction, command outcomes, and next step. For first OraReview creation, no existing review is expected; if orareview -x or orareview -t <txn> -x says no review exists after the ADE view, transaction, saved changes, and reviewers are verified, continue with CreateOra.sh and verify status after creation. When CodeSearch is unavailable or inconclusive, use the user's natural-language context and read-only inspection under /ade_autofs/ud522_fa/FUSIONAPPS_PT.V2MIBHCMBRONZE_LINUX.X64.rdd/LATEST/ for repository-relative paths before the ADE view exists. Use the repo-local scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper instead of the deployed ADE Lens RunInView.sh. The local wrapper enters the ADE view with an EOF/heredoc-style ade useview session, starts from the view's fusionapps root, can block while ADE prepares the view after cleanview, and should batch sequential ADE commands when there is no cleanview or approval boundary; required output checkpoints should be enforced inline inside the same loaded ADE session where practical. When preparing a command for this wrapper, if a repository-relative path starts with fusionapps/, pass it without the leading fusionapps/. For ADE view locks such as ade_info.vmd.free or Locked by process id, suspect ade persist first: run ade persist -list outside any view, close the matching full view with ade persist -c <viewname>, then retry once. Do not remove ade_locks.wrd automatically.
requirements: If ade-lens reports a newer version is available, install the update automatically before ADE work and continue without asking for approval.
Use the orareview skill for OraReview upload, status, close, and reviewer-comment workflows.
Use the mergereq skill for mergereq submission, quality, property, and post-approval merge workflows.
Use CodeSearch MCP for ADE/OpenGrok code searches.

When CodeSearch is unavailable or inconclusive, use natural-language context from the issue or follow-up and inspect repository-relative paths read-only under /ade_autofs/ud522_fa/FUSIONAPPS_PT.V2MIBHCMBRONZE_LINUX.X64.rdd/LATEST/ before the ADE view exists.
Use OraReview MCP tools for read-only review metadata, reviewer status, comments, and merge-check details.
Use Mergereq MCP tools for read-only report-card status and current-transaction quality previews.
Do not run ade_codex from the GitHub runner; this is already a non-interactive codex exec session.
Run ADE commands from the normal login home/ADE environment, not a temporary HOME.
For ADE view and transaction setup, prefer non-interactive ADE Lens scripts such as CreateViewAndTxn.sh, but use repo-local scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh for commands that enter an existing view.

scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh executes commands from the view's fusionapps root using EOF/heredoc-style ade useview; only when preparing a command for this local wrapper, if a repository-relative path starts with fusionapps/, pass it without the leading fusionapps/. Example: use hcm/per/db/plsql/per_asg_xla_pos_liquidation.pkb, not fusionapps/hcm/per/db/plsql/per_asg_xla_pos_liquidation.pkb.

If an approved ADE command fails with a view lock such as ade_info.vmd.free or Locked by process id, suspect ade persist before manual lock cleanup. From outside any view, run ade persist -list; if it lists the target full view name, run ade persist -c <viewname>, then retry the failed ADE command once. Do not remove ade_locks.wrd or ade_info.vmd.free automatically, and do not kill arbitrary PIDs. If ade persist -c does not clear the matching view or cleanup of .<hostname>_ade_server.sock/.<hostname>_ade_server.pid seems necessary, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input and ask for explicit approval.
If Create View has no transaction name, generate a simple transaction name from the view name and current UTC timestamp.
Create real ADE views in normal ADE storage so they are visible to ordinary ade lsviews.
If ADE falls back to /tmp/rnerolu_ade_* or reports sandbox write limits, stop and report failure instead of claiming the view was created.
Bind the work to exactly one ADE view.
Use ADE commands as the source of truth for view and transaction state.
Do not edit source files unless the additional inputs explicitly request source changes.

workflows.name: VB
workflows.description: Analyze and prepare VB/VBCS ALM Git bug fixes from natural-language instructions.
workflows.context: Plain-English VB/VBCS bug workflow for ALM Git projects. Treat the Task text as authoritative natural-language instructions and infer the bug number, base branch, important files, regression flag, requested checks, and desired output from the wording. First runs are read-only: use BugDB MCP for bug details, verify the selected local ALM Git checkout and requested base branch, inspect important files against origin/<base_branch>, and propose the exact branch, worktree, fix, test, push, and Draft ALM MR sequence with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input before changing state. After approval, keep the selected project checkout clean and create one per-bug branch plus one per-bug worktree under /scratch/<user>/codex-github-trigger/worktrees/<project>/<bug>/. Stop if the branch, remote branch, or worktree already exists unless the user approves reuse. For regression issues, inspect the last 30 first-parent merge commits on origin/<base_branch>, parse Merge-Request subjects, and rank likely culprit MRs by touched files and BugDB keywords. Use vblens-engineer and VBLens MCP for VBCS/VB code analysis, HTML/JavaScript review, MR issue retrieval, and component-aware guidance. Use ALM MCP default reviewers and create Draft merge requests through prepare_create_merge_request then confirm_create_merge_request after the approved fix branch is pushed.

workflows.name: ALM Backport
workflows.description: Backport one source BugDB bug's ALM transaction to a numeric maintenance release.
workflows.context: Approval-gated ALM Git release backport workflow. Use the selected local ALM Git project. Target releases must be numeric, such as 26.07, 26.10, 27.01, or compact 2607; bronze is current codeline and must stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. Derive maintenance branch, BugDB version, REL13PREARU tag, and branch naming from the target release. First run is read-only: read the source BugDB bug, locate exactly one ALM transaction/MR or ask for the MR URL, verify origin/maintenance-<release>, verify branch/worktree availability, and propose the numbered command sequence with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. After approval, create the backport bug, add REL13PREARU-<release> and FWD tags, run validation, cherry-pick into an isolated worktree branch, push, and create an ALM MR with explicit reviewers only: alok.x.sahay@oracle.com,sanket.jain@oracle.com,abhilash.chacko@oracle.com,mehul.shah@oracle.com. Do not use ALM default reviewers.

operations.name: Backport
operations.description: Prepare an approval-gated ALM backport for the source BugDB bug and target release.

workflows.name: Custom Commands
workflows.description: Run configured command intents or request approval for a natural-language command plan.
workflows.context: Custom command workflow. Use the selected run context and the validated command catalog. The runner starts GitHub Codex sessions with full local access on this trusted VM; the approval boundary is the issue workflow, command catalog, and follow-up approval comments. Catalog commands are approved to run in order. Direct natural-language catalog intents are approved when exactly one intent matches and required parameters are unambiguous. Skill updates are pre-approved for GitHub runner execution; if a selected skill reports a newer version, install it before continuing without asking for separate approval. For natural-language custom tasks, infer the relevant skill or MCP from the request text, project context, follow-up comments, and available ADE or transaction details. Use read-only MCP tools directly for information gathering, status, report-card, review-comment, and code-search questions. Use BugDB MCP first for existing bug reads, summaries, comments, and field updates when the needed MCP tool exists. If the MCP session cannot update existing bug fields, an approved BugDB field update may use the dev-bugdb-helper skill CLI at ~/.codex/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh. Use /bin/python3.11 only through that skill wrapper, do not change global python3, do not print OAuth tokens or client secrets, read the current bug/header fields before mutation, and resolve friendly field labels such as Bug Fix ETA, Bug Fix By, or Component Version to exact BugDB API keys before constructing the update payload. For product_id 2419, use cached metadata at docs/bugdb_header_fields_2419.json; call header-fields only if the cache is missing or does not contain the requested label. Do not guess field keys. Update only the requested fields while preserving required BugDB fields, then verify with a readback and rerun any gated validation. For first OraReview creation, no existing review is expected; if orareview -x or orareview -t <txn> -x says no review exists after the ADE view, transaction, saved changes, and reviewers are verified, continue with CreateOra.sh and verify status after creation. If CodeSearch is unavailable or inconclusive for ADE file discovery, use the user's supplied path or intent and read-only inspection under /ade_autofs/ud522_fa/FUSIONAPPS_PT.V2MIBHCMBRONZE_LINUX.X64.rdd/LATEST/ before the ADE view exists. Use the repo-local scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper instead of the deployed ADE Lens RunInView.sh. The local wrapper enters the ADE view with an EOF/heredoc-style ade useview session, starts from the view's fusionapps root, can block while ADE prepares the view after cleanview, and should batch sequential ADE commands when there is no cleanview or approval boundary; required output checkpoints should be enforced inline inside the same loaded ADE session where practical. When preparing a command for this wrapper, if a repository-relative path starts with fusionapps/, pass it without the leading fusionapps/. For ADE view locks such as ade_info.vmd.free or Locked by process id, suspect ade persist first: run ade persist -list outside any view, close the matching full view with ade persist -c <viewname>, then retry once. Do not remove ade_locks.wrd automatically. HCM bug validation blocks later work unless it exits 0 and outputs standalone PASS; non-PASS results require follow-up verification, BugDB updates through MCP when available or dev-bugdb-helper when MCP lacks field-update support, and validation rerun. HCM PMC must first identify the unique ADE view and transaction named rnerolu_bug-<bugid>, summarize the exact PMC command sequence, and ask for confirmation before running it. For natural-language state-changing requests without catalog commands, propose a numbered command sequence first and ask for approval with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. After approval arrives in a follow-up comment and the issue is tagged followup-codex, continue in the same session and run the approved sequence.

operations.name: Run Commands
operations.description: Run selected command-catalog intents, or propose and request approval for a natural-language command plan.
